definition.name: generalized_affine_map
definition.definition: Following Weis-Shirokov (2021, §2), a generalized affine map on a convex set K is a function f : K → R ∪ {+∞} such that f is affine on its effective domain dom(f) = {x ∈ K : f(x) < +∞}, i.e., f(λx + (1-λ)y) = λf(x) + (1-λ)f(y) for all x, y ∈ dom(f), 0 ≤ λ ≤ 1, and f is lower semicontinuous. The extension to +∞ is essential for handling unbounded observables on infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces (e.g., Hamiltonians H with Tr(Hρ) = +∞ for some density operators ρ). A sublevel set of f is {x ∈ K : f(x) ≤ β} and a level set is {x ∈ K : f(x) = β}. If all observables a_j ∈ A are bounded (i.e., belong to the algebra), then φ ↦ φ(a_j) is an ordinary (everywhere-finite) continuous affine functional, and the generalized theory reduces to the classical setting. Reference: Weis-Shirokov, J. Convex Anal. 28(3), 2021, Def. 1.
